title: Iria autumnalis
taxonomy: Iria autumnalis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Iria, which is a small group of plants native to the Mediterranean region. Iria autumnalis is often found growing in dry, rocky areas and is known for its small, yellow or white flowers. The plant is also sometimes referred to as autumnal Iria or autumn Iria, due to its tendency to bloom in the fall. Within the Asteraceae family, Iria autumnalis is closely related to other plants in the subfamily Carduoideae.
uses: Iria autumnalis, also known as autumn knapweed, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves, stems, and flowers are rich in compounds that have potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Some studies suggest that Iria autumnalis may be effective in reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, making it a potential natural remedy for skin issues and infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Iria autumnalis.
properties: Iria autumnalis, also known as Autumn Irises, have been used in traditional medicine for their potential therapeutic properties. The plant's roots and rhizomes are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. Iria autumnalis is said to have astringent and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Additionally, the plant's extracts may have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which could aid in wound healing and the prevention of infections. More research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Iria autumnalis.

parts: Iria autumnalis, also known as Autumn Squill, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The medicinal parts of the plant are the bulbs, which contain toxic compounds such as squill glycosides. These compounds have been traditionally used to treat conditions like respiratory issues and rheumatism. However, due to their toxicity, the bulbs should be handled with caution and only used under medical supervision. The bulbs contain other compounds like alkaloids and flavonoids, which may have additional medicinal properties.
intro:
Iris pseudacorus (also known as Yellow Flag Iris) and Iris pseudacorus subsp. (I. pseudacorus subsp. pseudacorus) (not Iria autumnalis) has been found to be confused with I. autumnalis. However, Iris autumnalis is a real plant and it is a perennial plant in the Iridaceae family.

Iris autumnalis is a plant used in herbal medicine. The health benefits of Iris autumnalis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridin, iridoids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Iris autumnalis include infusions, tinctures, and creams, which are used to treat various skin conditions and promote wound healing.
